sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
There are still 5 green nodes. The clicks didn't seem to change them permanently. Maybe the clicks toggled them but the clicks were on the wrong positions, or the mechanic is different. Let me think about this differently.
Looking at the image again at the first click result - I clicked at (19, 19) and the board changed. The first green plus at (1,1) position (1,1) in the top panel turned dark gray. Then I clicked the remaining 4 green nodes, and they all turned green again.
Wait - maybe the mechanic is that clicking a green plus toggles it, and clicking it again toggles it back. Or maybe the first click turned it dark gray, and then clicking the other 4 somehow reset everything.
Let me think about this more carefully. After the first click at (19, 19), the green node at that position turned dark gray. Then I clicked the remaining 4 green nodes at (19, 18), (19, 24), (19, 36), (25, 42), (37, 42). But the first green node was at (19, 19) and the center of the boundary [[18, 18], [18, 19], [19, 20], [20, 20], [21, 19], [21, 18], [20, 17], [19, 17]]. The center would be around (average) would be row = (18+18+19+20+21+21+20+19)/8 = 156/8 = 19.5, col = 19, col = (18+19+20+20+19+18+17+17)/8 = 148/8 = 18.5 = 18. So the center is (19, 18). But I clicked (19, 19) first. That's off by 1 column!
Then when I clicked the remaining 4, I used the computed centers which were (19, 18), (19, 24), (19, 36), (25, 42), (37, 42). The first one (19, 18) is the correct center of the first green node, which was already clicked and turned dark gray. So clicking it again probably toggled it back to green!
So the pattern is: clicking toggles the green plus shapes. The first click at (19, 19) was off by one column but still hit the green node. Then clicking the remaining 4 toggled them all of them.
Wait, but the result shows 5 green nodes still 5 green nodes. So either:
1. The first click at (19, 19) hit the green node and turned it dark gray, then clicking (19, 18) toggled it back to green.
2. Or the clicks don't actually toggle but do something else.
Let me look at the image more carefully. The image shows all 5 green plus shapes still green. So the clicks toggled them.
Hmm, but the goal might be to turn ALL of them dark gray. So I need to click each one exactly once. Let me try clicking all 5 at their correct centers: (19, 18), (19, 24), (19, 36), (25, 42), (37, 42).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click all 5 green nodes at their centers
centers = [(19, 18), (19, 24), (19, 36), (25, 42), (37, 42)]
action([{"action": "MOUSE", "row": r, "col": c} for r, c in centers])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
